fields.description:
Mirror how validity is produced, but enforce independence STRUCTURALLY: score novelty in a
SEPARATE Pass-2 structured sub-call that ALONE receives the prior findings and answers a
small novelty sub-answer set ("does this finding correspond to a prior finding?"). The
existing verification sub-call (severity attributes + binary/validity) receives NO prior
findings, so the `verify.py:64` independence invariant (VERIFIER_RULES[0]) holds by
construction. Pass-1 stays unanchored.

## Novelty sub-answer set + scoring arithmetic (canonical)

Novelty is the INVERSE of "matches a prior finding". The sub-call answers a small set of
factual **matches-prior** questions (yes / insufficient / no), each scored on the same
ordinal map Pass-3 uses for validity (`yes=1.0, insufficient=0.5, no=0.0`):

- Q1 — Does this finding restate the same underlying defect as a specific prior-review finding?
- Q2 — Does it cite the same plan location/section as that prior finding?
- Q3 — Is its suggested remediation substantively the same as that prior finding's?

`carryover_match = mean(answerable matches-prior sub-answers)`; **`novelty = 1 − carryover_match`** ∈ [0,1].
High novelty (≈1.0) = no prior match (genuinely new); low novelty (≈0.0) = carryover. The
questions are FACTUAL (match-prior), never normative ("should I downrank"). This mirrors how
validity = mean of the binary sub-answers, so Pass-3 maps novelty deterministically (task cc5b).

## Fallback (fail-safe — never silently suppress)

If the novelty sub-call errors, times out, or returns malformed/unparseable output, novelty
defaults to **0.0** (treated as carryover → enforced at the normal threshold, NEVER dropped).
A broken novelty signal can only make the gate STRICTER, never suppress a finding.

## `discriminates_novelty` eval gate (the bar — resolves the BLOCK)

Add a `discriminates_novelty` deterministic scorer mirroring `discriminates_true_from_false`
/ `discriminates_impact_levels` (eval_scorers.py): a PER-CASE boolean at the **0.5 midpoint**
— a `kind: novel` (`expect: high_novelty`) case must score novelty **≥ 0.5**; a
`kind: carryover` (`expect: low_novelty`) case **< 0.5**. Register it in the REGISTRY and add
labeled (prior-finding, new-finding) carryover/novel pairs to
`plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml` (≥ 3 pairs, both directions).

**The bar (numeric target):** the scorer is GATED by the spec-level convention already used by
the sibling discriminators — `gate: at_least(2)` over `epochs: 3` with `coverage_threshold: 0.8`
(i.e. every labeled novelty pair discriminated correctly at the 0.5 midpoint in ≥ 2 of 3
epochs). There is no separate AUC/F1 number: house style for deterministic discriminators is
the per-case 0.5 midpoint + the spec gate (the llm-judge `threshold:` field does NOT apply to
deterministic scorers). This eval bar is DISTINCT from the runtime drop threshold
`T_novel = 0.7` (task cc5b): 0.5 is "did the scorer separate novel from carryover at all"; 0.7
is "how novel must a finding be to be eligible for the Pass-3 drop". cc5b must NOT activate the
drop rule until this bar is met (the evidence gate).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Novelty scored in a separate Pass-2 sub-call that alone sees prior findings (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py`, `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py`)
- [ ] The severity/validity sub-call receives NO prior findings; Pass-1 receives none
- [ ] Novelty sub-answers are factual (match-prior), not "should I downrank"; `novelty = 1 − mean(matches-prior sub-answers)` on the `yes=1.0/insufficient=0.5/no=0.0` map
- [ ] Fail-safe fallback: novelty sub-call error/timeout/malformed → novelty = 0.0 (carryover; never dropped); covered by a test
- [ ] `discriminates_novelty` scorer (per-case, 0.5 midpoint) + ≥ 3 carryover/novel labeled pairs in `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml` + `eval_scorers.py` REGISTRY
- [ ] The bar is `gate: at_least(2)`/`epochs: 3` + `coverage_threshold: 0.8` on the novelty pairs (the framework idiom; NOT an AUC literal), distinct from the runtime `T_novel=0.7`
- [ ] The novelty scorer clears that bar before cc5b activates the drop rule (the evidence gate)
- [ ] A distinct registered structured-output contract for the novelty sub-call (e.g. `novelty_model` registered as `novelty`): fields for the matches-prior sub-answers (Q1/Q2/Q3 ∈ yes/insufficient/no) + the matched prior-finding id, a prompt id, registered alongside `verification` in `passes.py`/`verify.py` (the existing `verification` contract is NOT reused — it has no prior-findings input)
- [ ] `ALLOWED_EXPECTS` in `eval_scorers.py` is extended with a `NOVELTY_EXPECTS = {high_novelty, low_novelty}` set so `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` (`eval.py`) accepts the new dataset cases; the `discriminates_novelty` scorer keys off it
